title: CloudDash SDK Usage Guide
content:
CloudDash provides official SDKs for Python, Node.js, and Go to simplify API integration for dashboard management, alert configuration, and metric ingestion.

Python SDK: Install via pip install clouddash-sdk. Initialize with CloudDash(api_key=os.environ['CLOUDDASH_API_KEY']). Key methods: client.dashboards.list(), client.dashboards.create(name, widgets), client.alerts.create(name, condition, channels), client.metrics.ingest(data_points). The SDK handles pagination, rate limit retries, and typed response objects. Requires Python 3.9+.

Node.js SDK: Install via npm install @clouddash/sdk. Initialize with new CloudDash({ apiKey: process.env.CLOUDDASH_API_KEY }). Supports async/await and TypeScript definitions. Methods mirror the Python SDK with camelCase naming: client.dashboards.list(), client.alerts.create(), client.metrics.ingest(). Compatible with Node.js 18+.

Go SDK: Install via go get github.com/clouddash/clouddash-go. Initialize with clouddash.NewClient(os.Getenv('CLOUDDASH_API_KEY')). Uses context.Context for cancellation and timeout control. Ideal for high-throughput metric ingestion in Go microservices.

Authentication: All SDKs read CLOUDDASH_API_KEY from environment by default. Override with explicit api_key/apiKey parameter. For CI/CD, inject the key from your secrets manager as an environment variable.

Error Handling: SDKs raise typed exceptions/errors: AuthenticationError (401), RateLimitError (429, includes retry_after), NotFoundError (404), ValidationError (422 with field details). RateLimitError triggers automatic retry with backoff in all SDKs (configurable via max_retries parameter).

Pagination: List methods return paginated results. Python: for page in client.dashboards.list_paginated(): ... Node.js: for await (const dashboard of client.dashboards.list()) ... Go: use client.Dashboards.ListWithPagination(ctx, opts).

Terraform Provider: CloudDash maintains an official Terraform provider (clouddash/clouddash) for infrastructure-as-code management of dashboards, alert rules, and notification channels. See registry.terraform.io/providers/clouddash/clouddash for documentation.
